items.title: Father of last living US hostage in Gaza hopes Trump can bring his son home
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Unlike many families who blame Israel's government for not getting their loved ones released from captivity in Gaza, Adi Alexander is hesitant to point fingers. Pragmatic and measured, the father of the last living American being held hostage by Hamas just wants his son to come home.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Israeli military footage of strikes on targets in southern Lebanon
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Israel's military released on Saturday footage of strikes on targets in southern Lebanon in what was the heaviest exchange of fire since the ceasefire with Hezbollah started nearly four months ago.

items.title: Two dead as Israeli airstrikes pound Southern Lebanon in retaliation for rockets fired on Israel
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Israel struck Lebanon on Saturday in retaliation for rockets targeting Israel, killing two, including a child, in the heaviest exchange of fire since the ceasefire with the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah.

items.title: The AP goes behind the scenes at the Trevi Fountain
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Millions of tourists visit Rome’s Trevi Fountain every year, but most of them don’t know where the attraction’s water comes from. AP’s Trisha Thomas explains. #shorts #Rome #Italy #TreviFountain</article>

items.title: As Israeli bombs fell, wounded children overwhelmed this Gaza hospital. Dozens died
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>More than 300 casualties flooded into Khan Younis' Nasser Hospital after Israel shattered the ceasefire with a surprise barrage of airstrikes. What stunned doctors was the number of children.

(AP Video by Mohammad Jahjouh)

items.title: Hong Kong and Taipei mark Earth Hour by switching off lights
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Across the world on Saturday, lights are being switched off in cities as part of Earth Hour 2025. In Taiwan, the iconic Taipei 101 Building shut off its lights and in Hong Kong, skyscrapers by Victoria Harbour were plunged into darkness.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: At least 9 Palestinians killed in Gaza City after Israeli strikes on the territory
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>An Israeli airstrike killed at least nine Palestinians, including five members of the Al-Mashharawi family, in the Gaza Strip, hospital officials said Saturday. Another Israeli strike left structures used by local fishermen for homes and storing fish in ruins in central Gaza overnight.

items.content:

items.title: Flights resume at London's Heathrow Airport after 18-hour shutdown
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A fire at an electrical substation knocked out power to the airport for most of Friday, forcing Europe's busiest hub to shut down for roughly 18 hours, causing widespread cancellations and rerouting headaches, and stranding roughly 200,000 passengers.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Pumps in hidden chamber control water flowing to Rome's iconic Trevi fountain; AP explains
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Rome’s Trevi Fountain is one of Italy’s most popular monuments, but few visitors are aware of where its water comes from. The source is the Acqua Virgo, an ancient Roman aqueduct 16 kilometers (10 miles) from the city center and hidden behind the fountain is a manoeuvring chamber which controls the water coming in.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>
